content_en:
Du’aa’: Du’aa’ is always the weapon of the believer, whereby he asks Allaah to protect him from evil, trouble and bad behaviour and seeks refuge with Him from falling into the pit of kufr or wrongdoing because of anger. One of the three things that can help save him is: being fair at times of contentment and of anger.!!

One of the Du’aa’s of the Prophet 3 was:

“O Allaah, by Your knowledge of the Unseen and Your power over Your creation, keep me alive for as long as You know life is good for me, and cause me to die when You know death is good for me. O Allaah, | ask You to make me fear You in secret and in public, and | ask You to make me speak the truth in times of contentment and of anger. | ask You not to let me be extravagant in poverty or in prosperity. | ask You for continuous blessings, and for contentment that does not end. | ask You to let me accept Your decree, and for a good life after death. | ask You for the joy of seeing Your face and for the longing to meet You, without going through diseases and misguiding Fitnah (trials). O Allaah, adorn us with the adornment of faith and make us among those who are guided. Praise be to Allaah, the Lord of the Worlds.)

[62] sahih Al-Jaami’, 3039. [63] Arabic text and transliteration is as follows:

Allaahumma bi’ilmika -l-gaiba wa qudratika alaa -!-khalqi ahyinee maa alimta -l-hayaata khairan lee, wa tawaffanee izaa alimta -l-wafaata khairan lee, Allaahumma innee as’aluka khashyataka fee -I-gaibi wa - sh-shahaadah, wa as’aluka kalimata -I-haqqi fee -r-ridhaa wa -I-gadhab, wa as’aluka -I-qasda fee -I-ginaa wa -I-faqr, wa as‘aluka na’i‘eeman laa yanfad, wa as‘aluka qurrata ainin laa tangatiu, wa as‘aluka -r-ridhaa ba’ada -I-qadaa, wa as’aluka barda -I-aishi ba’ada -l-maw’t, wa as‘aluka ladzata -n-nadzari ilaa wajhika wa sh-shawga ilaa liqaa’ika fee gairi dharraa’a mudhirratin wa laa fitnatin mudhillatin, Allaahumma zayyinnaa bi zeenati -I-imaani wa -j-alnaa hudaatan muhtadeen. Alhamdu lillahi Rabbil aalameen.

When anger is successfully kept in check, the result is patience. If there is a strong recommendation in Islaam to check anger, by inference, there is an equally great emphasis on developing and strengthening patience.

Ways of strengthening patience

Allaah 4 has provided us with ways and means of obtaining and strengthening the quality of patience, for He has never instructed us to do something without providing us with help and support to do it, and ways and means of achieving it. He has never created a disease without creating a cure for it, and He has guaranteed cure when medicine is used.

Even so, patience is difficult, but it is not impossible to attain. Patience consists of two elements: knowledge and action, and from these two elements are derived the remedies for all spiritual and physical troubles. Knowledge and action, combined are always essential.

The element of knowledge is necessary in order to realize the benefits of following the commands of Allaah and the sense of happiness and fulfillment that one may attain by following them;

Golden Advice Series 03

and to understand what it is in the forbidden things that causes harm, imperfection and evil.

When a person realizes that, and adds strong will-power, the desire for spiritual achievement and the wish to live as a complete human being (as opposed to an animal-like existence given to rash and unreasonable reactions), then it will become easy for him to attain the quality of patience. The bitterness of patience will become sweet and the pain of patience will become joy.

Patience is a constant battle between the motives of reason and religion on the one hand, and the motive of whims and desires on the other. If a person wants reason and religion to overcome whims and desires, then he has to strengthen the former and weaken the latter, just like promoting good health and reducing the risk of illness.

Strengthening the motive of reason and religion

In the battle between reason/religion and whims/desires, we have the following “weapons” at our disposal.

Remember the glory of Allaah: We should remember the glory and greatness of Allaah, and feel that He is too great to be sinned against as He is All-Seeing and All-Hearing. Whoever thinks of the greatness of Allaah 4¢ will never be at ease in committing wrong actions in letting off his anger. Allah's Messenger 3 said:

“No one has swallowed back anything more excellent in the

Do not become Angry!

Resolve not to disobey Allaah: If we claim to love Allaah 48 and His Messenger 38, then we should not disobey them, because of that love. Allaah and His Prophet 38 have warned us against anger, and we should be obedient to the One we claim to love. Those who refrain from committing wrong action borne out of deep seated anger against another being because of their love for Allaah are of the highest status in His Sight, as are those who worship Him out of love for Him. There is a great difference between the one who obeys Allaah and abstains from wrong action out of love, and the one who does so out of fear of punishment.

Remember you are within the grip of Allaah: It may be possible that a person is encouraged into taking angry action against another person because of the power and control he has over him. However, one should call to mind that we are all in within the grip of Allaah.

Narrated by Abu Mas’ud Al-Ansaree 2s: “I was beating my slave with a whip when | heard a voice behind me:

Byers er i pat ‘Understand, Abu Mas’ud’;

but | did not recognise the voice due to intense anger. He (Abu Mas’ud) reported: As he came near me (I found) that he was the Messenger of Allaah 3 and he was saying:

“Bear in mind, Abu Mas’ud, bear in mind, Abu Mas’ud’.

He (Abu Mas’ud) said: | threw the whip from my hand. Thereupon he (the Prophet 3) said:

‘Bear in mind, Abu Mas‘ud; verily Allaah has more dominance upon you than you have upon your slave.’

| (then) said: | would never beat my servant in future.'*!

We should think of what the person who commits wrong action due to his anger has to lose, in this world and the next. It should be sufficient to think of the loss of Eemaan (faith), of which the smallest amount is worth much than everything in this world. How can anyone be willing to lose his Eemaan in return for some brief moment of just satisfying the urge to revenge.

“Be quick in the race for forgiveness from your Lord and for a garden whose width is that (of the whole) of the heavens and of the earth prepared for the righteous. Those who spend (freely) whether in prosperity or in adversity; who restrain anger and pardon (all) men; for Allaah loves those who do good.” [Aal-Imran (3):133-134]

[65] Muslim, Abu Dawood and Tirmidhee.

Do not become Angry!

Merely knowing the facts outlined in the preceding pages is not enough. We have to strive and do our utmost to achieve our aim and attain perfection. The best way to do so is to put a stop to the negative habits that are controlling our lives, as these are the main obstacles, which prevent us from succeeding.

VELA aul Allaahu Al-Musta‘an (And Allaah Is Our Source Of Help)

A Muslim has six obligations to another Muslim

An angel came down from Heaven and he was rejecting what he had As for what follows: Verily the most truthful speech is the Word of Avoid everything because of which one should apologize

Avoid suspicion, for suspicion is the gravest lie in talk and do not spy Bear in mind, Abu Mas‘ud; verily Allaah has more dominance

Did you kill him

Do not become angry

Do not become angry

Do not become angry

Do you intercede (with me) to violate one of the legal punishment

Do you seek to intervene concerning one of the punishments prescribed

Help your brother whether he is an oppressor or an oppressed

| guarantee a house in the surroundings of Paradise for a man who I guarantee a house in the surroundings of Paradise for a man who. | know a word which, if he were to say it, what he feels would go Ifa man gets angry and says, ‘I seek refuge with Allaah

If Allaah wants to do good to a person, He makes him comprehend If any of you becomes angry and he is standing, let him sit down

If any of you becomes angry, let him keep silent

If | were to say ‘Yes,’ it would become obligatory (for you to perform Indeed Allaah is beautiful and loves beauty. Kibr (pride) is to

It was because of this that those gone before you had perished Kindness is not to be found in anything but that it adds to its beauty Make your character good for the people, Muadh bin Jabal

No divorce and freeing of slave during annoyance

No one has swallowed back anything more excellent in the Sight of

Golden Advice Series 03

O Allaah, by Your knowledge of the Unseen and Your power over

O Haakim! This property is like a sweet fresh fruit; whoever

O Muslims! Allaah, Allaah! Do you call to (a thing pertaining to)

O people! Some of you make others dislike good deeds (the prayers) O people! The nations before you went astray because if a noble person People are like mines of gold and silver; those who were excellent

Shall | not tell you who is even stronger than him? The man who

Such people as paint these pictures will receive the severest punishment

The religion is Naseehah (sincere advice).” (Ad-Deen’ Naseehah)

The strong man is not the one who can overpower others (in wrestling) The strong person is not the one who is strong in wrestling

The strongest man is the one who, when he gets angry and his face The strongest tie of Eemaan (faith) is having loyalty for the sake There are three things which, whether undertaken seriously or in jest Verily the person who shall suffer the severest punishment

Whoever controls his anger at the time when he has the means Whoever controls his anger at the time when he has the means to Whoever has an atom worth of Kibr (pride) in his heart

Whoever seeks to please Allaah by making people angry

Whosoever wishes to advise the ruler concerning a matter

Aafat Alaa Tareeq (Obstacles on the Path of Da’wah) by Dr. Sayed Noah, Al-Mansura, Egypt: Dar Al-Yageen, 1421/2000.

Commentary on the Forty hadith of Al-Nawawi by Jamaal AdDin M. Zarabozo, USA: Al-Basheer Company for Publications and Translations, 1999.

www.Islamqa.com: How to handle Anger, (2001)
content_id:
Du'a: Du'a senantiasa menjadi senjata orang beriman, yang dengannya ia memohon kepada Allah agar melindunginya dari keburukan, kesulitan, dan perilaku buruk, serta berlindung kepada-Nya dari terjerumus ke dalam jurang kufr atau kezaliman akibat kemarahan. Salah satu dari tiga hal yang dapat menyelamatkannya adalah: bersikap adil baik dalam keadaan ridha maupun marah.

Di antara du'a Nabi ﷺ adalah:

"Ya Allah, dengan ilmu-Mu tentang yang gaib dan kekuasaan-Mu atas makhluk-Mu, hidupkanlah aku selama Engkau mengetahui kehidupan itu baik bagiku, dan wafatkanlah aku apabila Engkau mengetahui kematian itu baik bagiku. Ya Allah, aku memohon kepada-Mu agar menjadikanku takut kepada-Mu baik dalam keadaan tersembunyi maupun terang-terangan, dan aku memohon kepada-Mu agar menjadikanku berkata benar baik dalam keadaan ridha maupun marah. Aku memohon kepada-Mu agar tidak berlebihan baik dalam kefakiran maupun kekayaan. Aku memohon kepada-Mu kenikmatan yang tidak pernah habis, dan ketenangan hati yang tidak pernah terputus. Aku memohon kepada-Mu keridaan setelah ketetapan-Mu, dan kehidupan yang sejuk setelah kematian. Aku memohon kepada-Mu kelezatan memandang wajah-Mu dan kerinduan untuk bertemu dengan-Mu, tanpa melalui penyakit yang membahayakan dan fitnah yang menyesatkan. Ya Allah, hiasilah kami dengan hiasan iman dan jadikanlah kami pemberi petunjuk yang mendapat petunjuk. Segala puji bagi Allah, Rabb semesta alam."

[62] Sahih Al-Jaami', 3039. [63] Teks Arab dan transliterasinya adalah sebagai berikut:

Allaahumma bi'ilmika -l-gaiba wa qudratika alaa -!-khalqi ahyinee maa alimta -l-hayaata khairan lee, wa tawaffanee izaa alimta -l-wafaata khairan lee, Allaahumma innee as'aluka khashyataka fee -I-gaibi wa - sh-shahaadah, wa as'aluka kalimata -I-haqqi fee -r-ridhaa wa -I-gadhab, wa as'aluka -I-qasda fee -I-ginaa wa -I-faqr, wa as'aluka na'i'eeman laa yanfad, wa as'aluka qurrata ainin laa tangatiu, wa as'aluka -r-ridhaa ba'ada -I-qadaa, wa as'aluka barda -I-aishi ba'ada -l-maw't, wa as'aluka ladzata -n-nadzari ilaa wajhika wa sh-shawga ilaa liqaa'ika fee gairi dharraa'a mudhirratin wa laa fitnatin mudhillatin, Allaahumma zayyinnaa bi zeenati -I-imaani wa -j-alnaa hudaatan muhtadeen. Alhamdu lillahi Rabbil aalameen.

Ketika kemarahan berhasil dikendalikan, hasilnya adalah kesabaran. Jika dalam Islam terdapat anjuran yang kuat untuk mengendalikan kemarahan, maka secara tersirat terdapat pula penekanan yang sama besarnya untuk mengembangkan dan memperkuat kesabaran.

Cara-cara memperkuat kesabaran

Allah ﷻ telah menyediakan bagi kita cara dan sarana untuk memperoleh dan memperkuat sifat sabar, karena Dia tidak pernah memerintahkan kita melakukan sesuatu tanpa memberikan pertolongan dan dukungan untuk melakukannya, serta cara dan sarana untuk mencapainya. Dia tidak pernah menciptakan suatu penyakit tanpa menciptakan obatnya, dan Dia telah menjamin kesembuhan ketika obat digunakan.

Meskipun demikian, kesabaran memang sulit, tetapi bukan mustahil untuk dicapai. Kesabaran terdiri dari dua unsur: ilmu dan amal, dan dari kedua unsur inilah diperoleh obat bagi segala masalah rohani dan jasmani. Ilmu dan amal, keduanya digabungkan, selalu penting.

Unsur ilmu diperlukan untuk menyadari manfaat mengikuti perintah Allah dan rasa kebahagiaan serta kepuasan yang dapat diraih dengan mengikutinya;

Golden Advice Series 03

dan untuk memahami apa yang terdapat dalam hal-hal yang diharamkan yang menyebabkan bahaya, kekurangan, dan keburukan.

Ketika seseorang menyadari hal itu, dan menambahkannya dengan tekad yang kuat, keinginan untuk pencapaian spiritual, dan harapan untuk hidup sebagai manusia yang sempurna (berlawanan dengan kehidupan seperti binatang yang dikuasai oleh reaksi gegabah dan tidak masuk akal), maka akan menjadi mudah baginya untuk mencapai sifat sabar. Pahitnya kesabaran akan menjadi manis dan sakitnya kesabaran akan menjadi kegembiraan.

Kesabaran adalah pertempuran terus-menerus antara dorongan akal dan agama di satu sisi, dan dorongan hawa nafsu serta keinginan di sisi lain. Jika seseorang ingin akal dan agama mengalahkan hawa nafsu dan keinginan, maka ia harus memperkuat yang pertama dan melemahkan yang kedua, sama seperti meningkatkan kesehatan dan mengurangi risiko penyakit.

Memperkuat dorongan akal dan agama

Dalam pertempuran antara akal/agama dan hawa nafsu/keinginan, kita memiliki "senjata-senjata" berikut yang dapat kita gunakan.

Ingatlah keagungan Allah: Kita harus mengingat keagungan dan kebesaran Allah, dan merasa bahwa Dia terlalu agung untuk didurhakai karena Dia Maha Melihat dan Maha Mendengar. Barangsiapa memikirkan keagungan Allah ﷻ tidak akan pernah merasa tenang dalam melakukan perbuatan salah ketika melampiaskan kemarahannya. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda:

"Tidaklah seseorang menelan sesuatu yang lebih utama di sisi

Jangan Marah!

Bertekad untuk tidak mendurhakai Allah: Jika kita mengaku mencintai Allah ﷻ dan Rasul-Nya ﷺ, maka kita tidak boleh mendurhakai keduanya, karena kecintaan tersebut. Allah dan Nabi-Nya ﷺ telah memperingatkan kita dari kemarahan, dan kita harus taat kepada Dzat yang kita klaim untuk dicintai. Mereka yang menahan diri dari melakukan perbuatan salah yang timbul dari kemarahan mendalam terhadap orang lain karena kecintaan mereka kepada Allah berada pada kedudukan tertinggi di sisi-Nya, sebagaimana mereka yang beribadah kepada-Nya karena kecintaan kepada-Nya. Terdapat perbedaan besar antara orang yang taat kepada Allah dan menahan diri dari perbuatan salah karena cinta, dan orang yang melakukannya karena takut akan hukuman.

Ingatlah bahwa engkau berada dalam genggaman Allah: Mungkin saja seseorang terdorong untuk bertindak dengan kemarahan terhadap orang lain karena kekuasaan dan kendali yang dimilikinya atas orang tersebut. Namun, hendaknya ia mengingat bahwa kita semua berada dalam genggaman Allah.

Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Mas'ud Al-Anshari رضي الله عنه: "Aku sedang memukul budakku dengan cambuk ketika aku mendengar suara dari belakangku:

'Ketahuilah, Abu Mas'ud!';

tetapi aku tidak mengenali suara itu karena kemarahan yang sangat. Ia (Abu Mas'ud) melaporkan: Ketika ia mendekatiku (aku mendapati) bahwa ia adalah Rasulullah ﷺ dan beliau bersabda:

"Ketahuilah, Abu Mas'ud, ketahuilah, Abu Mas'ud."

Ia (Abu Mas'ud) berkata: Aku melemparkan cambuk dari tanganku. Kemudian beliau (Nabi ﷺ) bersabda:

'Ketahuilah, Abu Mas'ud; sesungguhnya Allah lebih berkuasa atasmu daripada engkau atas budakmu.'

Aku (lalu) berkata: Aku tidak akan pernah memukul pelayanku lagi di masa depan."

Kita harus memikirkan apa yang hilang dari orang yang melakukan perbuatan salah karena kemarahannya, baik di dunia maupun di akhirat. Seharusnya cukup untuk memikirkan hilangnya iman, yang jumlah terkecilnya pun lebih berharga daripada segala sesuatu di dunia ini. Bagaimana mungkin seseorang rela kehilangan imannya demi sekadar memuaskan keinginan balas dendam sesaat.

"Bersegeralah menuju ampunan dari Rabb-mu dan surga yang luasnya seluas langit dan bumi yang disediakan bagi orang-orang yang bertaqwa. (Yaitu) orang-orang yang berinfak baik di waktu lapang maupun sempit, dan orang-orang yang menahan amarahnya dan memaafkan (kesalahan) orang lain. Dan Allah mencintai orang-orang yang berbuat ihsan." [Aal-Imran (3):133-134]

[65] Muslim, Abu Dawood dan Tirmidhee.

Jangan Marah!

Sekadar mengetahui fakta-fakta yang diuraikan di halaman-halaman sebelumnya tidaklah cukup. Kita harus berusaha dan mengerahkan segala upaya untuk mencapai tujuan kita dan meraih kesempurnaan. Cara terbaik untuk melakukannya adalah menghentikan kebiasaan-kebiasaan negatif yang mengendalikan kehidupan kita, karena inilah rintangan utama yang menghalangi kita untuk berhasil.

Wallahu Al-Musta'an (Dan Allah-lah Tempat Memohon Pertolongan)

Seorang Muslim memiliki enam kewajiban terhadap Muslim lainnya

Seorang malaikat turun dari langit dan ia menolak apa yang telah Adapun selanjutnya: Sesungguhnya perkataan yang paling benar adalah Firman Allah Hindari segala sesuatu yang karenanya seseorang harus meminta maaf

Jauhilah prasangka, karena prasangka adalah ucapan dusta yang paling besar dan janganlah memata-matai Ketahuilah, Abu Mas'ud; sesungguhnya Allah lebih berkuasa

Apakah kamu membunuhnya

Jangan marah

Jangan marah

Jangan marah

Apakah kamu memberikan syafaat (kepadaku) untuk melanggar salah satu hukuman yang ditetapkan

Apakah kamu berusaha campur tangan mengenai salah satu hukuman yang ditetapkan

Tolonglah saudaramu baik ia seorang yang zalim maupun yang dizalimi

Aku menjamin sebuah rumah di sekitar surga bagi seorang yang Aku menjamin sebuah rumah di sekitar surga bagi seorang yang Aku mengetahui sebuah kalimat yang jika ia mengucapkannya, apa yang dirasakannya akan hilang Jika seseorang marah dan mengucapkan, 'Aku berlindung kepada Allah'

Jika Allah menghendaki kebaikan bagi seseorang, Dia menjadikannya paham Jika salah seorang dari kalian marah dan ia sedang berdiri, hendaklah ia duduk

Jika salah seorang dari kalian marah, hendaklah ia diam

Jika aku mengatakan 'Ya,' maka hal itu akan menjadi wajib (bagi kalian untuk melaksanakan Sesungguhnya Allah itu indah dan mencintai keindahan. Kibr (kesombongan) adalah

Karena hal inilah orang-orang sebelum kalian binasa Kelembutan tidaklah terdapat dalam sesuatu kecuali ia menambah keindahannya Perbaikilah akhlakmu terhadap manusia, wahai Muadh bin Jabal

Tidak ada talak dan pembebasan budak dalam keadaan marah

Tidaklah seseorang menelan sesuatu yang lebih utama di sisi

Golden Advice Series 03

Ya Allah, dengan ilmu-Mu tentang yang gaib dan kekuasaan-Mu atas

Wahai Haakim! Harta ini seperti buah segar yang manis; barangsiapa

Wahai kaum Muslimin! Allah, Allah! Apakah kalian menyeru kepada (sesuatu yang berkaitan dengan)

Wahai manusia! Sebagian dari kalian membuat orang lain tidak menyukai amal kebaikan (salat) Wahai manusia! Umat-umat sebelum kalian tersesat karena jika seorang yang mulia Manusia itu bagaikan tambang emas dan perak; mereka yang terbaik

Maukah aku beritahukan kepadamu siapa yang lebih kuat darinya? Seorang yang

Orang-orang yang menggambar gambar-gambar ini akan menerima hukuman yang paling berat

Agama itu adalah nasihat." (Ad-Deen Nasihat)

Orang kuat bukanlah orang yang dapat mengalahkan orang lain (dalam gulat) Orang kuat bukanlah orang yang kuat dalam gulat

Orang yang paling kuat adalah yang ketika ia marah dan wajahnya Ikatan iman yang paling kuat adalah loyalitas karena Allah Ada tiga hal yang, baik dilakukan dengan sungguh-sungguh maupun main-main Sesungguhnya orang yang akan menerima hukuman paling berat

Barangsiapa mengendalikan kemarahannya di saat ia mampu Barangsiapa mengendalikan kemarahannya di saat ia mampu untuk Barangsiapa memiliki seberat atom kibr (kesombongan) di dalam hatinya

Barangsiapa berusaha menyenangkan Allah dengan membuat manusia marah

Barangsiapa ingin menasihati penguasa mengenai suatu perkara

Aafat Alaa Tareeq (Rintangan di Jalan Dakwah) oleh Dr. Sayed Noah, Al-Mansura, Mesir: Dar Al-Yageen, 1421/2000.

Commentary on the Forty hadith of Al-Nawawi oleh Jamaal Ad-Din M. Zarabozo, USA: Al-Basheer Company for Publications and Translations, 1999.

www.Islamqa.com: How to handle Anger, (2001)
content_ar: Du’aa’: Du’aa’ is always the weapon of the المؤمن, whereby he asks Allaah to protect him from الشر, trouble and bad السلوك and seeks refuge with Him from falling into the pit of الكفر or الإساءة لأن of الغضب. One of the three things that can help save him is: being fair at times of contentment and of الغضب.!! One of the Du’aa’s of النبي ﷺ 3 was: “O Allaah, by Your العلم of the Unseen and Your power over Your creation, keep me alive for as long as You know الحياة is الخير for me, and cause me to die عندما You know الموت is الخير for me. O Allaah, | ask You to make me الخوف You in secret and in public, and | ask You to make me speak the الحق in times of contentment and of الغضب. | ask You not to let me be extravagant in الفقر or in prosperity. | ask You for continuous البركات, and for contentment that does not end. | ask You to let me accept Your decree, and for a الخير الحياة بعد الموت. | ask You for the joy of seeing Your face and for the longing to meet You, without going through diseases and misguiding Fitnah (الابتلاءات). O Allaah, adorn us with the adornment of الإيمان and make us among those who are guided. Praise be to Allaah, the Lord of the Worlds.) [62] صحيح Al-Jaami’, 3039. [63] Arabic text and transliteration is as follows: Allaahumma bi’ilmika -l-gaiba wa qudratika alaa -!-khalqi ahyinee maa alimta -l-hayaata khairan lee, wa tawaffanee izaa alimta -l-wafaata khairan lee, Allaahumma innee as’aluka khashyataka fee -I-gaibi wa - sh-shahaadah, wa as’aluka kalimata -I-haqqi fee -r-ridhaa wa -I-gadhab, wa as’aluka -I-qasda fee -I-ginaa wa -I-faqr, wa as‘aluka na’i‘eeman laa yanfad, wa as‘aluka qurrata ainin laa tangatiu, wa as‘aluka -r-ridhaa ba’ada -I-qadaa, wa as’aluka barda -I-aishi ba’ada -l-maw’t, wa as‘aluka ladzata -n-nadzari ilaa wajhika wa sh-shawga ilaa liqaa’ika fee gairi dharraa’a mudhirratin wa laa fitnatin mudhillatin, Allaahumma zayyinnaa bi zeenati -I-imaani wa -j-alnaa hudaatan muhtadeen. Alhamdu lillahi Rabbil aalameen. عندما الغضب is successfully kept in check, the result is الصبر. إذا there is a strong recommendation in Islaam to check الغضب, by inference, there is an equally great emphasis on developing and strengthening الصبر. Ways of strengthening الصبر Allaah 4 has provided us with ways and means of obtaining and strengthening the quality of الصبر, for He has never instructed us to do something without providing us with help and support to do it, and ways and means of achieving it. He has never created a المرض without creating a الدواء for it, and He has guaranteed الدواء عندما medicine is used. Even لذا, الصبر is difficult, but it is not impossible to attain. الصبر consists of two elements: العلم and action, and from these two elements are derived the remedies for all spiritual and physical troubles. العلم and action, combined are always الجوهري. The element of العلم is necessary من أجل realize the benefits of following the commands of Allaah and the sense of happiness and fulfillment that one may attain by following them; Golden Advice Series 03 and لفهم what it is in the forbidden things that causes harm, imperfection and الشر. عندما a person realizes that, and adds strong will-power, the desire for spiritual achievement and the wish to live as a complete human being (as opposed to an animal-like existence given to rash and unreasonable reactions), then it will become easy for him to attain the quality of الصبر. The bitterness of الصبر will become sweet and the الألم of الصبر will become joy. الصبر is a constant battle between the motives of العقل and religion on the one hand, and the motive of whims and desires on the other. إذا a person wants العقل and religion to overcome whims and desires, then he has to strengthen the former and weaken the latter, just like promoting الخير الصحة and reducing the risk of المرض. Strengthening the motive of العقل and religion In the battle between العقل/religion and whims/desires, we have the following “weapons” at our disposal. Remember the glory of Allaah: ينبغي لنا remember the glory and greatness of Allaah, and feel that He is أيضاً great to be sinned against as He is All-Seeing and All-Hearing. Whoever thinks of the greatness of Allaah 4¢ will never be at اليسر in committing الخطأ actions in letting off his الغضب. الله's Messenger 3 said: “No one has swallowed back anything more excellent in the Do not become Angry! Resolve not to disobey Allaah: إذا we claim to المحبة Allaah 48 and His Messenger 38, then ينبغي لنا not disobey them, لأن of that المحبة. Allaah and His النبي 38 have warned us against الغضب, and ينبغي لنا be obedient to the One we claim to المحبة. Those who refrain from committing الخطأ action borne out of deep seated الغضب against another being لأن of their المحبة for Allaah are of the highest status in His Sight, as are those who worship Him out of المحبة for Him. There is a great difference between the one who obeys Allaah and abstains from الخطأ action out of المحبة, and the one who does لذا out of الخوف of العقاب. Remember you are within the grip of Allaah: It may be possible that a person is encouraged into taking angry action against another person لأن of the power and control he has over him. غير أن, ينبغي للمرء call to العقل that we are all in within the grip of Allaah. Narrated by Abu Mas’ud Al-Ansaree 2s: “I was beating my slave with a whip عندما | heard a voice behind me: Byers er i pat ‘Understand, Abu Mas’ud’; but | did not recognise the voice بسبب intense الغضب. He (Abu Mas’ud) reported: As he came near me (I found) that he was الرسول ﷺ of Allaah 3 and he was saying: “Bear in العقل, Abu Mas’ud, bear in العقل, Abu Mas’ud’. He (Abu Mas’ud) said: | threw the whip from my hand. Thereupon he (النبي ﷺ 3) said: ‘Bear in العقل, Abu Mas‘ud; verily Allaah has more dominance upon you than you have upon your slave.’ | (then) said: | would never beat my servant in future.'*! ينبغي لنا think of what the person who commits الخطأ action بسبب his الغضب has to lose, in this الدنيا and the next. It should be sufficient to think of the loss of Eemaan (الإيمان), of which the smallest amount is worth much than everything in this الدنيا. How can anyone be willing to lose his Eemaan in return for some brief moment of just satisfying the urge to revenge. “Be quick in the race for المغفرة from your Lord and for a garden whose width is that (of the whole) of the heavens and of the earth prepared for the الصالح. Those who spend (freely) whether in prosperity or in adversity; who restrain الغضب and pardon (all) men; for Allaah loves those who do الخير.” [Aal-Imran (3):133-134] [65] مسلم, أبو داود and Tirmidhee. Do not become Angry! Merely knowing the facts outlined in the preceding pages is not enough. We have to strive and do our utmost لتحقيق our aim and attain perfection. The best الطريق to do لذا is to put a stop to the negative habits that are controlling our lives, as these are the الرئيسي obstacles, which prevent us from succeeding.
